title: Aegoseris setosa
intro: Aegoseris setosa, also known as Yarrow, is an annual or perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its fern-like foliage and small white or yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and promoting wound healing due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Yarrow include essential oils, sesquiterpene lactones, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Yarrow include teas, infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, often used to treat minor injuries, skin irritations, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Aegoseris setosa is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it among the flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. Aegoseris setosa is further classified within the genus Aegoseris, which is a small group of annual plants native to the southwestern United States. The species setosa is a subset within this genus, characterized by its unique characteristics and growth habits. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Aegoseris setosa exhibits the typical characteristics of this plant family, including composite flowers and a central disc of smaller flowers.
